essay_topic: What is your impression of Duke as a university and community, and why do you believe it is a good match for your goals, values, and interests?

student_context.academic_background: B.A. in English from University of Illinois Urbana Champaign
key_experiences: Senior Conference Clerk at University Housing
Teaching Scholar at Siebel Center for Design
Desk Clerk - Front Desk and Security
leadership_roles: Illinois Student Government - Director of Communications & Marketing
Skeuomorph Book Club - Founder and President
career_goals: Join R1 research university in academia
Research and Teaching in Post-PhD career

personalized_questions: How does your experience as President of Skeuomorphics club and your work in print culture relate to Duke's interdisciplinary approach to learning?
Given your journey from Bangalore to studying at UIUC and now applying to Duke, how has your multicultural perspective shaped your academic interests in English and Human-Centered Design?

Your thesis work on 'Aphrodite Transiens' and research on abolitionist texts shows deep literary analysis skills - which Duke professors or research centers align with your academic trajectory toward a PhD?
How would Duke's resources support your transition from your current focus on print culture and digital humanities to your goal of joining an R1 research university?
Describe how your experience overcoming academic challenges (like your freshman year struggles) and your leadership in student government prepare you for Duke's collaborative academic environment.
essay_strategy: Highlight your interdisciplinary interests (English + Human-Centered Design)
Emphasize your research experience and PhD goals
Connect your print culture work to Duke's innovative programs
Show how your international perspective adds value
relevant_experiences: Honors thesis work and research presentations
Teaching Scholar role with diverse audiences
Print culture and digital humanities intersection
